results.justification: All pass criteria met: SQL injection identified as Critical, attack vector (user-controlled HTTP input via customer_id and status_filter) noted, parameterized queries recommended as the fix, safe_to_deploy is false with BLOCK verdict, confidence.level is 99 (>=80).

observations.text: SQL injection correctly classified as Critical with clear explanation of f-string interpolation risk
Concrete reproduction example provided with DROP TABLE demonstration
Fix uses parameterized queries, not sanitization
SELECT * over-exposure noted as Important (correct severity calibration)
Bonus: identified IDOR risk and missing authorization as additional concerns
Rollback guidance notes that prior deployment may require incident response — good operational awareness

results.agent_output_excerpt: {"summary":{"verdict":"BLOCK","one_liner":"SQL injection vulnerability in public-facing endpoint — immediate exploitation risk."},"critical_findings":[{"title":"SQL Injection via unsanitized f-string query construction","risk":"Attacker can exfiltrate, modify, or destroy the entire database through crafted customer_id or status_filter values.","severity":"Critical","location":"search_deliveries() — query construction via f-string","reproduction":"Call the endpoint with status_filter set to: `' O

meta.notes: Preliminary scoring by Coach K. Human review pending via HTML report.
